title: carya tomentosa var. subcoriacea
intro: Carya tomentosa var. subcoriacea, also known as the hickory tree, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bark has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the bark is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption.
